title: Experiment Record (PROTOCOL.md §14.1, Protocol 0.5 sharded ledger)
description: One immutable JSON file per experiment at state/ledger/<id>.json. A correction is a NEW record referencing the prior id in parent_ids; records are never mutated. additionalProperties is intentionally true: consumers (e.g. ActivityEncoder) carry additive fields like maturity_level, not_deployable, artifacts.mlflow, factorial_cells, and schema-validity is necessary but not sufficient for verifier-completeness.

anyOf.description: New (Level-1 content-addressed) provenance shape — the full triple, so the visible reachability bit is never silently dropped.

protocol_version.description: Protocol version stamp; MUST be the literal "0.5" for Protocol 0.5 records.

id.description: YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-<6hex>[-<slug>]; unique per record and the ledger filename stem.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of record creation.

parent_ids.description: ids of parent records, or empty for a root. The sentinel "baseline" is also accepted by validate_ledger.py.

status.description: Free-form status label (e.g. baseline, promising, branch_winner, failed, infra_failed, budget_truncated, invalid, informative_failure, rejected).

failure_reason.description: Required by the reference validator when status is infra_failed or budget_truncated; recommended for invalid.

coordinator_executor_separation.description: Optional §5.8 achieved separation level for Research Director / Implementation Worker handoff evidence.

lifecycle_status.description: OPTIONAL tree lifecycle state for derived research_tree views. Distinct from evidence/promotion labels in status/promotion_status.

promotion_status.description: OPTIONAL promotion/evidence label used by derived tree views. Existing status labels keep their protocol meaning; this field separates promotion posture from lifecycle state.

frontier_eligible.description: OPTIONAL override for whether this node belongs in the derived active frontier view. When omitted, regenerate_state.py derives frontier eligibility from lifecycle_status.

blocked_by.description: OPTIONAL blockers for lifecycle_status=blocked. Values may be record ids, issue ids, or human-decision markers.

merged_into.description: OPTIONAL target record id when lifecycle_status=merged.

node_type.description: OPTIONAL tree node kind for derived research_tree views.

branch_insights.description: OPTIONAL propagated branch insights. Unlike local lessons[], these are compact, ledger-traceable observations meant to update ancestor constraints or future branch choices.

raw_observation.description: Measured result, failure, or event observed in the run.

distilled_insight.description: Short interpretation of why the observation matters for the branch.

source_record_ids.description: Immutable ledger ids that support this insight.

updates_parent_ids.description: Ancestor/root node ids whose future constraints or choices this insight updates. The baseline sentinel may be used for root-level constraints.

validated_constraint.description: Future proposal constraint inferred from the evidence.

invalidated_ideas.description: Sibling ideas, mechanisms, or proposal shapes no longer worth pursuing without new evidence.

confidence.description: Confidence in this propagated insight based on evidence quality and maturity level.

retirement_signal.description: Condition that should trigger reconsidering this insight.

review_status.description: Whether Skeptic, human, or another review path has challenged or accepted the insight.

review_record_ids.description: Optional ledger ids or review-bearing records that reviewed or challenged the insight.

source_commit.description: Content-addressed-provenance Level-1 breadcrumb: the commit HEAD pointed at when this experiment ran. NON-AUTHORITATIVE — a hint, not a reproducibility guarantee. Off-main experiment branches and squash-merges mean it may never resolve from main; see resolvable_from_main. Auditability is carried by the structured record (hypothesis/params/metrics/lineage), not by this pointer. See docs/proposals/2026-06-13-provenance-redesign.md.

source_branch.description: The git branch the experiment ran on (e.g. an off-main campaign branch). Records where source_commit lived when captured.

resolvable_from_main.description: True only if source_commit is an ancestor of main. False for off-main/ephemeral-branch experiments; makes the (un)auditability of the commit pointer VISIBLE rather than an implied promise.

git_sha_before.description: DEPRECATED (Protocol 0.5 -> content-addressed provenance). Superseded by source_commit + resolvable_from_main. Accepted for back-compat with pre-existing records; new records MUST NOT emit it.

metrics.description: Measured metrics for this run; may be empty for preflight/admin records.

data_fingerprint.description: OPTIONAL split/dataset identity for this run (PROTOCOL.md §6.3.1, §14.1). Records WHICH split the run used so 'same comparison set' is explicit and checkable across a baseline/candidate pair (verifier rule 11_comparison_set_identity, warn-not-gate). Recommended but NOT required (back-compat: existing records stay valid) and NOT forced via anyOf. Depth is project-chosen: the strongest form carries per-split membership_sha256 (byte-level proof); a lighter form carries dataset_fingerprint + split_spec_hash + seed (assumes deterministic materialization). Either is accepted.

mode.description: Which §6.3.1 split mode produced this run's data: frozen | declarative.

dataset_fingerprint.description: Guard B fingerprint of the dataset. Identity is (source, version, date_window) — required for a comparable identity; row_count + schema_hash are optional strengtheners (omit for a growing dataset identified by date range). See split_manifest.schema.json.

split_spec_hash.description: Hash of the split rule/spec (and seed) used to materialize the partition. Lighter same-set proof when the materialization is deterministic.

seed.description: Seed used to materialize a declarative split.

val_set_version.description: val_set_version this run evaluated against (§17.6); part of split identity.

membership_sha256.description: OPTIONAL strongest-tier proof: per-split sha256 over the sorted membership ids, computed at run time (byte-level 'same set' proof even without frozen files).

node_title.description: OPTIONAL curated per-node title used to derive research_tree.json node content.

node_lessons.description: OPTIONAL curated per-node narrative lessons used to derive research_tree.json node content.

val_queries_incurred_by_this_run.description: Primary val-exposure derivation input (PROTOCOL §17.6). Non-negative: a negative count must not cancel ledger-derived exposure in the verifier's anti-spoof sum.
